headlines.title: Royal Marines board Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in English Channel
headlines.description: Marines were joined by National Crime Agency officers in the six-hour long operation in the early hours of Sunday.

headlines.title: Watch: MOD video shows Russian shadow fleet tanker interception
headlines.description: British armed forces boarded a Russian shadow fleet oil tanker in the English Channel.

headlines.title: What does 1-0 win mean for Scotland's chances of qualification?
headlines.description: With eight third-place finishers advancing to the last 32, might three points be enough for Scotland to make history?

headlines.title: Tech firms had 'enough time' says Nandy, ahead of social media announcement
headlines.description: The prime minister is expected to announce measures on protecting teenagers online on Monday.

headlines.title: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed on Sunday as Tehran casts doubt on timing
headlines.description: The US president's comments come as Iran says an exact date has not been decided.

headlines.title: Jarvis says Labour must 'meet the moment' on defence spending
headlines.description: The new defence secretary tells the Sunday Telegraph he is determined to ensure the military gets "what they need".

headlines.title: Jubilation as Scotland fans celebrate win
headlines.description: Supporters gathered in bars and venues across the country in the early hours to watch the match against Haiti.

headlines.title: Why Haiti v Scotland was antidote to the ills of world football
headlines.description: It might have been nervy. It might not have been pretty. But Scotland are here. And Scotland have won. Tom English reports from Boston.

headlines.title: Two men charged over theft of England World Cup kit
headlines.description: Two men are charged for stealing $18,000 worth of property from England at their Kansas City training base.

headlines.title: Smaller than Isle of Man & huge Dutch influence: Curacao making history
headlines.description: Curacao has possibly been most famous for its alcoholic drink of the same name - but now the nation is ready to announce itself at the World Cup.

headlines.title: Remarkable story of refugee who quit Bayern and became Australia's record-breaker
headlines.description: Nestory Irankunda was born as a refugee in Tanzania before becoming Australian record breaker with goal in victory over Turkey.

headlines.title: Has Vinicius Jr brilliance just papered over the Brazilian cracks?
headlines.description: Vinicius Jr scores a wonder goal to rescue his side but Brazil's performance against Morocco raises more questions than answers.

headlines.title: New rules abound - was VAR diving decision wrong despite feeling right?
headlines.description: Some new rules and tweaks to a few old ones have been introduced for the 2026 World Cup. But are the changes leading to confusion?

headlines.title: Iran win four staff visa appeals but 11  banned
headlines.description: Four members of Iran's World Cup delegation win appeals against having their visa applications rejected but 11 staff remain banned from travelling to the United States.

headlines.title: Why football is called 'soccer' in the US and Canada
headlines.description: Football is life for millions of fans around the world, but in two World Cup co-host nations, they tend to call it by a different name.

headlines.title: How Myles Smith turned five years of therapy notes into a debut album
headlines.description: The singer songwriter's first collection is inspired by the experiences that shaped him before he was famous.

headlines.title: Vincent's parents 'never say he's good enough' - so he turned to a middle-aged couple online
headlines.description: A niche group of content creators called "virtual parents" have become hugely popular among young people.

headlines.title: Are the Downing Street dominoes about to fall?
headlines.description: John Healey's resignation as defence secretary is a disaster for the prime minister, writes Laura Kuenssberg.

headlines.title: Licence to thrill: The making of the James Bond video game soundtrack
headlines.description: The BBC went behind the scenes as the score for the blockbuster James Bond video game was recorded.

headlines.title: As Trump turns 80, what's it really like to work as an octogenarian?
headlines.description: As President Donald Trump celebrates his birthday, we ask people who have worked into their 80s themselves about what it takes to keep going.

headlines.title: Norway braces for verdict in rape trial of crown princess's son Høiby
headlines.description: Marius Borg Høiby will appear via video link, almost three months after his trial came to an end on 40 charges, including four counts of rape.

headlines.title: Why the US economy keeps defying the odds
headlines.description: Why has the American economy continued to outperform so many of its peers, despite facing the same global shocks?

headlines.title: Resident doctors cancel strike after new offer from government
headlines.description: The walkout had been due to start at 07:00 BST on Monday and last until Friday.

headlines.title: New York Knicks win NBA championship for first time in over 50 years
headlines.description: The Knicks clinched the title against the San Antonio Spurs, 94-90, in the fifth game of the best-of-seven series.

headlines.title: Russian families use AI to 'resurrect' loved ones killed in Ukraine
headlines.description: The highly controversial trend lies at the intersection of Russia's war on Ukraine, new AI technologies and grief.

headlines.title: Does the UK have a blind spot on UFO sightings?
headlines.description: A campaigner is calling on the government to resume investigations into alleged encounters.

headlines.title: Woman fearful of 'predators' before her death, family say
headlines.description: Police are reviewing the investigation into Katie Beattie's death following calls from her family.

headlines.title: Dig site offers 'window' into Roman life
headlines.description: A decorative flagon in the shape of a woman's head is among the finds.

headlines.title: Welsh church in village home to legendary hound voted 'best place on Earth' by Reddit users
headlines.description: St Mary's Church in Beddgelert was the last place standing in a challenge that started with a world map.

headlines.title: Card games replace smartphones after school ban
headlines.description: A Coventry head teacher says pupils are having chats and playing games after they banned phones.

headlines.title: Watch: Truck carrying fireworks catches fire, sparking spectacular display
headlines.description: No one was harmed in the incident, which saw motorists treated to an early Fourth of July show.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: By-Electioncast: Could Restore Britain Help Andy Burnham Win?
headlines.description: A new poll suggests the party could steal a significant number of voters from Reform UK.

headlines.title: The battle in rural America against AI data centres
headlines.description: Utah approves the world’s largest data centre (62sq miles) but opposition is growing

headlines.description: It might have been nervy. It might not have been pretty. But Scotland are here. And Scotland have won. Tom English reports from Boston.

headlines.title: What does 1-0 win mean for Scotland's chances of qualification?
headlines.description: With eight third-place finishers advancing to the last 32, might three points be enough for Scotland to make history?

headlines.title: Fifa to pay Somali referee Artan full World Cup fee
headlines.description: Somali referee Omar Artan, who was denied entry to the United States to officiate at the World Cup, will still receive his tournament fee from Fifa in full.

headlines.title: Refugee who quit Bayern to create Australia World Cup dream
headlines.description: Nestory Irankunda was born as a refugee in Tanzania before becoming Australian record breaker with goal in victory over Turkey.

headlines.title: Knicks end 53-year wait for NBA Championship
headlines.description: The New York Knicks win their first NBA Championship since 1973 thanks to a 94-90 win over the San Antonio Spurs.

headlines.title: Has Vinicius Jr brilliance just papered over the Brazilian cracks?
headlines.description: Vinicius Jr scores a wonder goal to rescue his side but Brazil's performance against Morocco raises more questions than answers.
